name: Cleft Palate and Lip
description: Cleft palate is a congenital failure of fusion of the hard and/or soft palate, and cleft lip is a corresponding defect of the upper lip. These defects cause milk to enter the nasal passages, leading to aspiration pneumonia in affected neonates.
symptoms: Milk bubbling from nostrils when nursing
treatment_overview: Surgical repair of the palate is performed at 3-4 months. Prior to surgery, tube feeding is essential to prevent aspiration. Prognosis is good with successful surgical repair.
prevention_tips: Avoid nutritional deficiencies and certain drugs during pregnancy
Do not breed from lines with known cleft incidence
